meta.demoDay: Final Demo Day #2 + Graduation · Jul 23–24, 2026 · NYC (the convent)
meta.tagline: a 10-week program turning cryptography and AI research into products people can use.
meta.runBy: IC3 × Flashbots × the convent × blockchain builders fund

journey.icp: TEE and confidential-data teams that need formal verification or registry-grade proof of deployment properties

journey.solution: a formal-verification registry and proof workflow for dstack/TEE Postgres-style deployments
journey.nextMilestone: validate the registry spec against TeeSQL or another live TEE database project

journey.solution: a P2P LLM router plus x402-kit for agent-native payments and gateway composition

teams.name: Contexto

journey.icp: long-running agent builders who need memory, episode retrieval, and context observability across runtimes — with a contested reframe toward mass-market personal-assistant users
journey.problem: agents lose task continuity when context collapses, moves across tools, or becomes impossible to inspect
journey.solution: an episode-based context engine that instruments memory and retrieval across agent runtimes
journey.nextMilestone: ship a personal-assistant PoC and run enough executive-assistant discovery interviews to settle the contested wedge

journey.icp: wallet and cross-chain teams that need policy-constrained signing without encumbering user keys
journey.problem: cross-chain asset movement creates signing, custody, and key-encumbrance risks that are hard to express safely in UX
journey.solution: a Liquefaction-inspired wallet prototype with signing committee and cross-chain flow experiments
journey.nextMilestone: demonstrate one policy-constrained signing flow that a real wallet user can understand

teams.name: Daedalus
teams.traction: Cornell Tech / ex-Aptos / Jane Street
teams.now: scraping Polymarket L2/L3 orderbook history to backtest delta-neutral stock-portfolio hedges — new demo targeted by end of next week
seeking: feedback from cohort teams building trading or agent execution systems
integration partners for protected order flow primitives
offering: market microstructure learnings (toxic flow handling, latency, orderbook dynamics)
open data + evaluation pipeline (orderbook ingestion, backtesting)
paperBasis: PROF — Protected Order Flow in a Profit-Seeking World (arXiv 2510.15205)
priorShipping: PROF paper (arXiv 2510.15205) — protected order flow mechanism design
Real market data + live trading validation in progress

journey.icp: systematic prediction-market traders and infrastructure builders who need protected order-flow evaluation
journey.problem: prediction-market liquidity and execution quality are constrained by toxic flow, latency, and weak microstructure tooling
journey.solution: a discovery tool for delta-neutral hedges (Polymarket + sports events vs stock portfolios) — intelligence, not execution; gated on rigorous correlation backtesting before any app is built
journey.nextMilestone: show one correlation backtest that validates (or kills) the delta-neutral hedge thesis — demo targeted by 2026-06-19

teams.about: Prediction-market microstructure research — a liquidity-and-intelligence engine for prediction markets and InfoFi. Building AI-powered market-making infrastructure that combines predictive intelligence, calibrated belief models, and systematic execution into a unified data, risk, and execution layer for institutional and systematic traders. Research lineage in the PROF paper (IC3). daedalus-research.com.

teams.name: DCNet
teams.now: exploring DCNet anonymous-broadcast designs for blockchain coordination and private web search
seeking: protocol reviewers for dining-cryptographer-style anonymity tradeoffs
product collaborators who can pressure-test private-search and blockchain use cases
offering: anonymity-network design review
private broadcast primitives for cohort projects that need coordination without attribution leakage

journey.icp: protocol builders who need anonymous broadcast or private group coordination
journey.problem: useful coordination often reveals who spoke, searched, or initiated a message even when payloads are encrypted
journey.solution: productize DCNet-style anonymous broadcast for blockchain and private-search settings
journey.nextMilestone: publish a minimal architecture note or demo showing the anonymity/scalability tradeoff

teams.about: DCNets, _Dining Cryptographers Networks_, are overlay networking protocols for _anonymous broadcast_ of messages. Multiple promising architectures have been proposed, with varying tradeoffs for scalability and trust. This team will explore the state-of-the-art and productization of the technology, with applications to blockchains and private web search.

journey.icp: agent builders who need verifiable AI-to-AI contracts with on-chain escrow

journey.nextMilestone: turn the negotiation product + PiCred provenance into a CVM API-platform PoC and land the first external discovery conversation

Eliza Cloud — managed backend for hosted agents (auth, billing, container deploys)
Desktop (Electrobun) and mobile clients with local-first agent runtimes

journey.icp: developers and teams building agentic applications that need a runtime, plugin system, and hosted deployment path
journey.problem: agent builders repeatedly rebuild runtime, connector, memory, and deployment plumbing before reaching product work
journey.solution: an open-source agentic fullstack operating system with runtime, cloud, desktop, mobile, and plugin surfaces
journey.nextMilestone: define the buyer and last-mile distribution — scope the MVP (cloud, multiplayer, power users) and test a non-technical co-founder GTM motion

journey.icp: people who need repeatable practice for interviews, pitches, meetings, and high-pressure speaking
journey.problem: people consume information constantly but rarely practice articulating it out loud before it matters
journey.solution: short speaking reps with AI scoring, content feedback, and targeted next-practice suggestions

teams.name: Feedling

teams.about: Information-diet interventions for short-form video. Uses TEE-delegated access to a person's watch history to reshape what their feed serves — putting the user, not the platform, in control of their information diet.

teams.name: searxng-wth-frnds
teams.traction: deployed private-LLM-search prototype benchmarked at 10k queries — median +1.2s latency, p99 ~9s
teams.now: private LLM search with metadata privacy via flashnet — exploring reputation-gated egress to dodge Tor-exit blocking and IP poisoning

feedback on what should stay local, what should sync, and what should never leave the LAN

journey.icp: small trusted groups that need private shared search without a hosted search backend
journey.problem: useful group search often leaks private queries, sources, or social context into centralized infrastructure
journey.solution: a LAN-first peer search daemon built around SearXNG and trusted local peers
journey.nextMilestone: one real cohort workflow using private search, plus a minimal anonymity architecture note for the reputation-gated egress design

teams.about: A LAN-first peer search daemon — local-first, privacy-by-default search shared across a local network of trusted peers. Built on the SearXNG metasearch engine.

teams.name: Shake
teams.traction: 150+ users, pre-monetization

journey.icp: people at events or dense social spaces who want a quick mutual connection without exchanging handles manually
journey.problem: IRL social connection still has awkward manual exchange and weak memory after the moment passes
journey.solution: an iOS social app where a shared phone-shake gesture creates the connection
journey.nextMilestone: test whether repeated event use creates second-session behavior rather than one-time novelty

teams.about: A consumer social app that connects people when they physically shake their phones together — turning a shared gesture into a connection. iOS (Shake Social).

teams.name: Shape Rotator OS
teams.focus: cohort coordination layer · cohort viewer + context vault
teams.traction: first outside cohort-project contribution merged (Teleport Router, PR by James Barnes)
teams.now: re-architected for outside contributions — hunting real user stories for product-driven development

named user stories — what should the OS answer for you on a normal day?

journey.icp: cohorts and residencies that need a shared coordination surface — people, projects, timelines, and context in one local-first viewer
journey.problem: cohort knowledge lives in scattered transcripts, profiles, and chats, so coordination work stays invisible and unqueryable
journey.solution: an open, local-first cohort OS — surface data, context vault, and timeline views — that outside projects can merge into
journey.nextMilestone: open real cohort workflows to outside contributions and ship the next updates against named user stories

teams.about: The cohort's own coordination layer and viewer (dmarzzz/shape-rotator-os): cohort surface data, context vault, timelines, and program views in one local-first Electron app, with a public web mirror at os-web.shaperotator.xyz. Re-architected in week 3 to take outside contributions — the Teleport Router merge was the first cohort-project-to-OS integration.

teams.name: Trustless TEE
teams.now: clarifying the TEE trust-root problem rather than treating vendor signing keys as invisible infrastructure
seeking: TEE builders to pressure-test where trust actually enters their stack
protocol reviewers who can separate enclave proof from hardware-root trust
offering: sharp trust-boundary review for TEE-based projects
Ethereum execution context around latency, sequencing, and priority updates

journey.icp: TEE protocol builders who need a clearer answer to the vendor-key trust assumption
journey.problem: confidential-compute designs can inherit a hidden trust root from Intel/AMD signing keys even when the rest of the protocol is trust-minimized
journey.solution: make that trust root explicit and explore a trustless or less-vendor-dependent TEE design
journey.nextMilestone: publish or demo the minimum concrete trust-root alternative the cohort can inspect

teams.about: Trustless trusted-execution. Addresses the trust-root problem that confidential-compute systems inherit — the hardware vendor's signing key — rather than treating it as a given.

clusters.name: agentic-dev-platform
clusters.label: agentic coding + memory infrastructure
clusters.description: Layers of agentic dev infra — JJHub is a jj-native forge for agent-generated code; Contexto manages memory/context for long-running agent processes; Bitrouter routes inference across agents. Together — a complete stack for building AI-native dev teams. Workshop candidate — "building with the agentic forge".

clusters.name: agents
clusters.label: agent runtime
clusters.description: Agent runtime stack — JJHub harness, Contexto context engine, Bitrouter routing, Conclave skills runtime, and elizaOS runtime/plugin substrate. Composable; workshop candidate.

clusters.name: confidential-ai-ops
clusters.label: attestable AI inside TEEs
clusters.description: Teams working on attestable AI or confidential execution workflows. Prova targets healthcare prior-auth; DealProof targets negotiation; SignalStack targets privacy comms; Conclave targets confidential dataset evaluation; Trustless TEE and TvEE RL sit on the trusted-execution and attested workflow side of the same primitive. All could benefit from shared attestation, logging, and enclave-operating patterns.

clusters.name: confidential-data
clusters.label: data procurement + attestation
clusters.description: Marketplaces where data buyers + sellers transact inside TEEs without revealing raw data. Prova provides TLSNotary-backed data integrity; DealProof adds dual-agent negotiation; TeeSQL + Tinycloud provide the storage and delegation primitives. Shared vertical surface — healthcare/research dataset procurement, confidential vendor negotiations.

clusters.name: consumer-behavior-apps
clusters.label: consumer behavior + feedback apps
clusters.description: Consumer-facing applications that turn user behavior into feedback loops: Elocute for speaking practice with scored feedback, Feedling for information-diet interventions around short-form video, and Shake as a social app surface.

clusters.name: crypto-id
clusters.label: crypto / identity research
clusters.description: Crypto + identity research axis — Pramaana lattice/ASC, Crossroads Liquefaction, WikiGen bio-cryptography. Could share an IC3 mentor pairing.

clusters.name: dstack
clusters.label: dstack / TEE-postgres
clusters.description: TEE Postgres axis — TeeSQL builds it, Abra integrates, Tinycloud + Conclave deploy.

clusters.name: local-first-networking
clusters.label: local-first / peer networking
clusters.description: Peer, local-first, and cross-network primitives: DCNet explores dining-cryptographer-style anonymity, searxng-wth-frnds is a LAN-first peer search daemon, and Teleport Router works on cross-network routing.

clusters.name: market-mechanism-research
clusters.label: markets / mechanism research
clusters.description: Market and mechanism-design work spanning prediction market microstructure, routing, and Liquefaction-style crypto research. Daedalus is the focal market-microstructure project; Crossroads and Bitrouter are adjacent mechanism and routing substrates.

clusters.name: ndai
clusters.label: NDAI lineage
clusters.description: Three teams share the NDAI paper — Conclave framework, Prova sandbox marketplace, DealProof dual-agent. Risk of stepping on each other; DealProof flagged Prova as collaboration target.

clusters.name: realtime-creative
clusters.label: voice-to-output generative stage
clusters.description: Three teams converge on a runtime — capture voice/context, process via agents (Contexto memory), output live generatives (Etherea). Tinycloud provides the sovereign data backbone. Natural demo-day artifact — accelerator uses this for live pitch backdrops and agent-assisted curation.

clusters.name: regulated
clusters.label: healthcare / regulated
clusters.description: Compliance-driven enterprise as a shared GTM target buyer — Prova EHR pilot, Abra SOC2/SOX, SignalStack privacy NGOs/journalists, and Enclave medical data sharing.

dependencies.reason: Abra is building a formal-verification registry around dstack TEE Postgres and explicitly asks for TEE Postgres beta access; TeeSQL is the cohort team offering TEE Postgres service and onboarding.

Bitrouter is opening an agentic payment/LLM-routing toolkit and asks for feedback from projects in agent harnesses; JJHub is the cohort's agentic coding platform and is seeking DX feedback from teams building with LLM-as-primitive.

Bitrouter and TeeSQL both lean on TEE remote attestation as an admission/trust primitive that displaces reputation — Bitrouter for permissionless LLM-provider onboarding, TeeSQL for attestation-gated cluster membership.

Conclave's confidential-signal pipeline needs long-horizon memory (keeping early-cohort signal relevant weeks later), which is exactly Contexto's relevant-subtree context-injection capability; both teams already list each other as dependencies.

Conclave and Teleport Router build on the same substrate — an open-source, policy-enforcing agent running inside a TEE that processes private data from mutually-distrusting parties and emits only scoped, derived output — applied to organizer-signal mining vs ambient session routing.
Contexto is looking for long-running agent dogfooders and observability pairs; JJHub is shipping an agentic coding platform and seeking cohort teams to dogfood Smithers for agentic CI.

Contexto (ingest long-horizon context) and Teleport Router (ambient cross-pollination feed) are adjacent stages of the same goal — removing manual prompt relay between collaborators' agents; Teleport Router already lists contexto as a dependency.

Pramaana offers a post-quantum identity layer and SDK for cohort tools; Crossroads is building a wallet and asks for cross-chain/policy-constrained signing feedback. The public records support product complementarity around identity and wallet flows.
Conclave is factoring a reusable TEE-agent skill framework while Prova is building a healthcare AI agent and offering a trust API for other cohort teams.
Both projects expose dual-attestation / contract-layer primitives for agent workflows, and DealProof's weekly goal names ProvaTrust attestation-layer complementarity as a conversation to pursue.
Prova and SignalStack are both shipping TEE-backed AI workflows and both ask for comparison around deployment patterns and attestation UX.

Prova is hardening TEE attestation for a healthcare AI agent while TeeSQL is onboarding cohort teams to confidential SQL on dstack; the public source supports shared TEE substrate, not a confirmed product dependency.

Shape Rotator OS can consume Teleport Router's shared-notebook / cross-pollination feed (exposed as a plain REST API) instead of reimplementing the cohort introduction pipeline; the OS already lists teleport-router as a dependency and the router is the first cohort project merged into the OS.

Tinycloud and TeeSQL both operate around dstack TEEs, scoped delegation, confidential infrastructure, and cohort-facing services, but the current public records support substrate overlap rather than a confirmed product dependency.

awardCategories.label: Best Shape Rotation
awardCategories.description: The most substantiated pivot toward product-market fit over the program — a team that changed shape in response to real feedback and can show it.

awardCategories.label: Best Team–Product Fit
awardCategories.description: The clearest evidence that this is the right team for this problem, drawn from how they work, what they ship, and how they take feedback.
